20min.description: Blood pressure and heart rate drop to normal levels. Circulation in hands and feet already improves.

8h.description: Nicotine and carbon monoxide levels in the blood drop by half. Blood oxygen starts to normalize.

12h.description: Carbon monoxide completely eliminated from the blood. All cells in the body receive more oxygen.

24h.description: The risk of heart attack starts to decrease. Early withdrawal anxiety and irritability tend to pass.

48h.description: Nerve endings damaged by tobacco begin to recover. Smell and taste become noticeably sharper.

3d.description: Nicotine completely eliminated from the body. Bronchial tubes relax — breathing becomes visibly easier. Energy increases.

1w.description: Blood is less viscous. Peripheral circulation improves — hands and feet become warmer. Skin more oxygenated.

2w.description: Circulation improves noticeably and lung capacity can increase by up to 30%. Physical exercise becomes easier.

3w.description: Circulation in the limbs stabilizes. Walking long distances without getting so tired is already noticeable.

1m.description: Lung cilia begin to regenerate. Productive coughing decreases, the lungs clean themselves more efficiently.

3m.description: Blood circulation substantially improved. Risk of thrombosis decreases. Lung function continues to improve.

6m.description: Lung cilia fully regenerated — mucus and residue are removed normally. Wheezing and shortness of breath reduce greatly.

9m.description: Lungs almost completely recovered. Respiratory infections (bronchitis, flu) become much less frequent.

1y.description: Risk of coronary heart disease drops by 50% compared to an active smoker. An extraordinary scientific milestone.

2y.description: Risk of heart attack approaches that of someone who never smoked. Coronary arteries function like those of a non-smoker.

5y.description: Stroke risk reduces to the level of non-smokers. Risk of mouth and throat cancers also falls by half.

10y.description: Lung cancer risk drops by half. Risk of larynx, esophagus, and bladder cancers also decrease significantly.

15y.description: Risk of coronary heart disease equal to that of someone who never smoked. 15 years of choices that changed everything.

20y.description: Risk of pancreatic, kidney, bladder, and esophageal cancers equals that of non-smokers. You completely transformed your health.

diary.title: Diary
diary.emptyText: When you feel like smoking, record it here. This helps you understand your patterns.

modal.title: Record craving
modal.notesPh: Describe how you're feeling right now...
modal.howManyCigs: How many cigarettes did you smoke?

stats.title: Statistics
stats.fewData: Not enough data yet — keep it up!
stats.cravingsByWeekday: CRAVINGS BY DAY OF THE WEEK
stats.hardestDay: {{day}} is the hardest day — stay alert.
stats.noCravings: No cravings recorded in this period.
stats.noRelapses: No relapses recorded. Keep it up! 🌟
stats.relapseInsightSuffix: Even so, you avoided {{rate}}% of the cigarettes you would have smoked. That is real progress.

settings.title: Profile
settings.logoutMessage: Your diary and progress are saved. If you sign in again, everything will be here.

sos.title:
It will pass.
Choose how
to hold on:
sos.subtitle: Cravings last, on average, 3 minutes. Let's get through this together.
sos.recordSubtitle: How are you feeling right now?
sos.alreadyPassed: Already passed? Back to the app

breathePhases.inspireInstruction: Breathe in deeply through your nose...

sos.breatheDoneSub:
You completed all 3 cycles.
How are you feeling?
sos.waitMsg1: Breathe deeply. You are in control.
sos.waitMsg3: Almost there. The craving is fading.
sos.waitDoneSub:
The craving passed.
You are stronger than it.
sos.recordNotesPh: Describe how you're feeling right now...
sos.savedCededSub: Note what happened and move forward.

relapse.title:
It happens.
Don't give up.
relapse.subtitle: A relapse doesn't erase the progress you've already made. What matters is what you decide now.
relapse.howMany: HOW MANY CIGARETTES DID YOU SMOKE?
relapse.resetSubtitle: I want to start over honestly
relapse.continueJourney: It was just a slip and I continue
relapse.continueSubtitle: Record the moment but keep my date
relapse.footnote: Whatever your choice, we are here with you. ❤️
relapse.confirmResetSub: Your counter will reset and restart from today. Your history and diary remain saved.
relapse.recordedSub: Your relapse has been noted in the history. Your quit date stays the same. Keep going!

welcome.headline: Breathe again.
welcome.subtitle: Your journey to quit smoking starts with one step. We walk with you.

profile.title: Let's get to know you
profile.subtitle: Your data stays only on your device and helps personalize your experience.
profile.privacy: 🔒 Your data is never shared with third parties.

when.title: When did you quit?
when.subtitle: This date is the starting point for everything. It can even be today!
when.infoText: 🎯 If you quit a while ago, put in the real date. Your milestones and metrics will be calculated from there.

consumption.title: Tell us about your consumption
consumption.subtitle: We'll use this to calculate your savings and health progress.
consumption.calcYearSuffix: on cigarettes. In 1 year that is

triggers.title: What are your triggers?
triggers.subtitle: Select the situations that most make you want to smoke. This helps us support you at the right moments.

daily.title: Libre 🌿
messages: Another day building your best self. You can do it! 💪
Every hour without smoking is a victory. Keep it up! 🌱
Your body is thanking you right now. ❤️
Today is another day of freedom. You chose well! ✨
You are stronger than the craving. Remember that! 🛡️
Every cigarette not smoked is a gift to yourself. 🎁
Breathe deeply. You are on the right path. 🫁

craving.title: You usually crave now 🫁
craving.body: How about doing a breathing exercise first? You are ready!

quotes: The craving lasts minutes. Freedom lasts forever.
You are not losing something. You are gaining your health back.
Every cigarette not smoked is a victory over addiction.
When the urge hits, remember: you are stronger than it.
Your future thanks you for every hour you resist.
It's not about perfection. It's about persistence.
Nicotine lies. It tells you that you need it. That's not true.
The urge passes in 3 to 5 minutes. Breathe deeply and wait.
You are rewriting your story, paragraph by paragraph.
Every morning without a cigarette is a morning with cleaner lungs.
Difficult days exist. But you have gotten through all of them so far.
The craving is strong. You are stronger.
One step at a time. One day at a time. That is enough.
The money you saved tells a story of choice.
Smoking was a habit. Not smoking is who you are becoming.
Breathe. This cleaner air is yours by right.
The urge passes. The pride of having resisted stays.
There is no such thing as just one cigarette. There is your freedom or going back to the start.
You are proving, every hour, that you can do it.
Your heart pumps cleaner blood today than yesterday.
Every meal you taste more fully is a gift from the addiction you beat.
You are not a smoker who quit. You are a free person.
With every craving beaten, you become a little more free.
Today you chose yourself. That matters more than it seems.
Your body is smarter than the addiction. Give it time.
Taking care of yourself starts with the decision you already made.
Freedom is not the absence of desire. It is choosing not to give in to it.
Every hour counts. Every day counts. Every choice counts.
You deserve a life without depending on anything to feel good.
Cigarettes promised to relieve stress. You found better ways.
